title: Open batch onboarding and device-side SNMP trap target scope
releaseGate: New owner-requested scope after the single-device server/network syslog closure. This batch must not reopen the old batch-011 blocker; it owns explicit batch onboarding and device-side SNMP trap target delivery.

stories.title: Add explicit batch onboarding API contract
stories.scope: Add backend batch onboarding plan/ensure endpoints for explicit `codes[]`. Execute sequentially by default, reuse the single-device action/evidence contract per code, and return aggregate counts plus per-device results without raw controller bodies.
nonGoals: Do not execute devices selected by current filters.
Do not add remote parallel execution.
Do not change the single-device API response contract.
acceptance: Batch plan and ensure routes accept `codes[]` and reject empty or oversized requests.
Response includes per-device results and aggregate summary counts.
Server monitor-mode requirements are preserved per device.
Focused backend tests cover success, partial failure, and request validation.
notes: Keep the first implementation sequential and blast-radius-limited.

stories.title: Wire guarded batch onboarding UI
stories.scope: Update the Device V2 redesigned page so multi-select `继续纳管` uses the batch API, shows a guarded confirmation, makes server monitor-mode policy explicit, and renders per-device results after execution.
nonGoals: Do not run batch onboarding from filters.
Do not redesign the page shell.
Do not hide mixed success/failure results behind one success toast.
acceptance: Selecting multiple rows no longer hits the single-device warning path.
Confirmation displays target count and monitor-mode policy.
Per-device result rows render success/failed/unknown/blocked truth.
Typecheck passes.
notes: Use Chrome devtools for the final UI check when a local UI target is available.
Do not trigger broad live remote runs from the browser without bounded evidence intent.

stories.title: Run all-device collection validation gate
stories.scope: Before device-side trap target work, run a full explicit-code Device V2 store validation pass for every current device. Record per-device success/blocked truth and preserve expected unreachable devices as exact per-device blockers, not a global execution failure.
nonGoals: Do not force unreachable devices to pass.
Do not start trap target delivery before this gate is recorded.
Do not persist credential material in evidence.
acceptance: All current Device V2 codes are included explicitly in one store/start validation task.
Aggregate task result and per-device statuses are recorded.
Unreachable or non-detectable devices are recorded as expected blockers with controller snapshot anchors.
The story chain clearly gates SNMP trap target work on this collection validation evidence.
validation: POST /api/v1/device/v2/store/start with all current codes and options.device_collection2_mode=dc2_only
notes: Owner explicitly warned that some devices are expected to be unreachable.

stories.title: Define SNMP trap target profile commands
stories.scope: Add the device-side SNMP trap target command/profile layer for H3C/Comware. Produce commands to configure trap host, save config, and verify readback; unsupported profiles must return exact blockers.
nonGoals: Do not assert trap arrival at the collector.
Do not touch collector-side listener publish code.
acceptance: H3C/Comware trap target commands are deterministic and unit-tested.
Default trap listener port handling is explicit.
Unsupported vendor/platform returns a blocked action with template identity.
No credential material is persisted.
notes: Prefer the existing network syslog profile pattern.
D2ON-046 only defines and unit-tests the command/profile layer; controller execution remains D2ON-047.

stories.title: Execute SNMP trap target through controller
stories.scope: Connect the H3C/Comware SNMP trap target profile to onboarding ensure. Resolve managed `snmp_trap_listener`, call controller `/api/v1/remote/run`, persist status/exit/readback summaries, and keep syslog evidence separate.
nonGoals: Do not store raw controller responses.
Do not make SNMP trap target success required for syslog success.
Do not add batch remote parallelism.
acceptance: Onboarding action evidence includes `snmp_trap_target` truth.
Controller success and verify-match produce configured status.
Controller/runtime failures produce failed or unknown retryable status.
Focused backend tests pass.
notes: Use the same secret-free evidence discipline as D2ON-039/D2ON-040.
If no managed trap listener is available, record the exact blocker.

stories.title: Run bounded batch and trap live probes
stories.scope: Run one bounded validation pass: a small explicit-code batch onboarding probe and one H3C/Comware device-side SNMP trap target probe. Persist exact evidence and update readiness for the new scope only.
nonGoals: Do not run broad fleet onboarding.
Do not claim all-vendor SNMP trap support.
Do not reopen the old single-device syslog closure.
acceptance: Evidence records selected device codes and why the probe is bounded.
Batch response preserves per-device truth and aggregate counts.
SNMP trap target result is success or exact blocker with readback details.
Final readiness states what remains out of scope.
notes: Use `oneops_test_tool` and Chrome devtools only for bounded checks.
Do not write credentials into evidence.
